title: Limnochloa quadrangulata
taxonomy: Limnochloa quadrangulata is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. This species belongs to the genus Limnochloa and is a type of aquatic grass. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and Australia. Limnochloa quadrangulata is often found growing in wetlands, rivers, and streams. The plant's classification is further detailed in the Linnaean system, which categorizes it as Limnochloa quadrangulata.

intro: Limnochloa quadrangulata is a plant species commonly known as "Water Plantain" or "Bog Asphodel," and is a type of aquatic plant found in wetlands and bogs. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Limnochloa quadrangulata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
